features.summary: Costco reported Q4 2024 net income of $2.35B ($5.29 per share), up 9% year-over-year. Net sales reached $78.2B with comparable sales up 5.4% globally (6.9% adjusted for gas/FX). Traffic increased 6.4% worldwide while average ticket declined 0.9%. The company opened 30 new warehouses in fiscal 2024 and plans 29 openings in 2025. E-commerce grew 18.9% with strong performance in appliances and big/bulky items. Membership fee increases took effect September 1st, with renewal rates at 92.9% in US/Canada.
features.management_outlook: Management expects continued momentum with 26 net new warehouse openings planned for fiscal 2025, including 12 international locations. They anticipate strong growth in e-commerce and big/bulky logistics, which delivered 4.5M items (up 29%). Technology investments including membership card scanners and app improvements are enhancing member experience. The company sees significant opportunities in retail media revenue streams and continues expanding Kirkland Signature penetration in the high-20% range.
features.risks: Port strikes pose potential disruption to non-food imports, though management has implemented contingency plans including pre-shipping and alternate routing. Red Sea shipping delays continue causing minor disruptions. Supply constraints persist in eggs due to avian influenza and some beef/vegetable SKUs remain tight. Ocean freight rate volatility could impact costs, though most freight is under contract. Interest income will be a headwind in H1 2025 due to lower cash balances and rates.
features.key_drivers: Strong traffic growth of 6.4% worldwide driven by membership card scanner rollout improving checkout efficiency and member experience. Non-food categories led growth with double-digit increases in jewelry, gift cards, toys, and home furnishings. Fresh departments showed high single-digit growth with successful price investments driving volume. Kirkland Signature continues outpacing overall business growth with new product launches and localized production reducing costs while improving member value.
